transcript.text: Hello, this is Living in Boulder Vlog with me, the 24th of October, 2012, 2013.
Today I went to work, and then after that I got high with some dudes, and then rode my bike home,
And there's this Trevor who's here, who smoked up.
He stopped by a couple months ago. He's going to live in his van with his girlfriend.
And then I went to the pet store, and I got new chips for my hamsters. I had to get new chips.
Oh, and I got some E2 chip now. Wee. I got chips for my hamsters.
I got another house for that hamster, just in case they want to have their own house.
But they like to sleep with each other, so I'm curious to see what they'll do.
That guy's still eating, and then just put that thing in the middle.
I think I'm going to get another water thing now, but then I got a couple of geckos. Check this out.
They live like 15 years or something, get up to 10 inches.
They eat these little bugs, so I just throw the bugs in and the lettuce to feed the bugs,
And when they get hungry, they eat the bugs, and there's two of them.
So, and the other geckos in there, I have to spray the inside and make it really moist,
And they lick it and stuff, and then I have to change out the water every day.
But other than that, they're pretty simple.
And then I have all my crickets here.
I got like 250 crickets in there.
And I got, because they hide in these little tubes.
Like, if you look in the tube, I don't have a light around with me,
But if you look in the tube, there's like crickets lining.
Oh, you can see crickets lining all down these tubes.
And then they come out and eat the lettuce.
I think I spray, you know, humidity in there too for them to,
And then, yeah, they throw them in there.
So that'll be cool.
And I'm going to read the guide today.
But, yeah, that's what I did today.
Peace out.
